title: Cholesterol condensing effect on DPPC bilayer (LIPID21, 325 K)

C1.note: Two bilayers built with packmol-memgen + LIPID21/TIP3P. tLEaP succeeded: system/dppc/leap.log and dppc_chol/leap.log both 'Errors = 0', topologies written (bilayer_only_lipid.top: 8.9MB/8.76MB). Atom counts match report (31732 pure DPPC; 29586 DPPC:chol). Target composition met: 94 DPPC + 40 CHL = 70:30 (vs prompt ~90 DPPC + 38 chol); net charge 0; boxes ~62x62x79 / 60x60x79 A. Split-residue warnings are normal LIPID21 head/tail decomposition, no residues dropped.

C2.note: Tier protocol followed per parameter: FF LIPID21+TIP3P justified (Dickson JCTC 2022 PMID 35113553, RAG p.50); Berendsen barostat (ntp=1, taup=1.0) justified for lipids vs MC-deforms-bilayer (RAG p.50/396); cut=10 A, ntt=3, temp0=325 K (above DPPC Tm~314 K -> fluid Lalpha) each justified. No hardcoded defaults. PLAN/PROCESS/STUDY reports present; pure-DPPC reference added to make 'condensing' comparative.

C3.note: min1/min2/heat/chunked-equil/equil2/prod all finished both systems. Both productions ran full 100 ns (nstlim=5e7, dt=0.002): prod.mdout has 'Final Performance Info' + 'Total wall time: ~9 h'. Density stable 0.997-0.998 g/cc, T=325.3/325.4 K, no blow-up/NaN. APL timeseries flat to 99.99 ns (apl_SYS{A,B}.dat).

C4.note: Correct observables for the prompt: area-per-lipid from NetCDF box cell_lengths (apl.py) and deuterium order parameters via cpptraj 'lipidscd' (sys{A,B}/scd.in, scd_dppc.dat). Both computed against a matched pure-DPPC reference under identical settings; per-carbon Scd profiles + comparison plots produced. Analysis window last 80 ns with block-SEM convergence check.

C5.note: Expected (key, paper-confirmed): APL decreases to ~50-55 A2 vs ~63 pure DPPC; Scd increases; cholesterol OH at interface. Agent: SYS-B APL 53.60 A2 (squarely in 50-55 window), SYS-A 65.94 A2 (~2-3 A2 above exp, acknowledged LIPID21 overestimate), dAPL -18.7%; plateau |Scd| 0.175->0.219 (+25%), higher at every carbon. Both quantitative condensing signatures match strongly; cholesterol OH-at-interface (3rd key item) not explicitly computed but is structurally guaranteed by setup. 0.95 for the un-quantified orientation item. PAPER-READ METHOD: exact source (Rog JPCB 2007, 111:7886, DOI 10.1021/jp072969q) is not in Europe PMC and ACS full text was paywalled (doi.org 404, pubs.acs.org 403 via WebFetch). Key's claims confirmed instead against the same Rog group's primary MD literature retrieved via mcp__pubmed: Rog & Pasenkiewicz-Gierula 2006 (PMID 16920840, cholesterol OH anchored at bilayer interface + chain ordering) and Rog et al. 2007 (PMID 17293396, 'ordering and condensing effects of sterols'), plus Smith & Freed 2012 (PMID 22586732, cholesterol condensing effect). Answer-key direction and magnitude corroborated.
